findings.message: New write endpoint archive_item has no structured logging, no domain-level metric, and no explicit trace span — it is an uninstrumented code path. The generic ObservabilityMiddleware counts HTTP-level requests, but a state-mutating business operation (archive) has no log line tying the action to a correlation id / request id, no counter (e.g. items_archived_total), and no span attributes (item_id). Operators cannot answer 'how many archives happened, by whom, and which ones failed' from /metrics or logs.
findings.suggestion: Inject the bound logger (demo.logging_config.get_logger) and log 'item_archived' with item_id and the correlation id at the success path; add an explicit counter for archive operations to the MetricsRegistry (or via recoverability-style module metric) and expose it in /metrics; if OTel is enabled, set span attributes (item.id, action='archive') on the current span.

findings.message: 404 error path (item not found) raises HTTPException without logging — the error is not recorded with the correlation id, so a spike in archive-not-found is invisible in logs and is not surfaced as an error metric (HTTPException 404s are not 5xx and therefore won't increment app_request_errors_total). This violates the 'error paths logged with correlation id' contract.
findings.suggestion: Before raising, emit a warning-level structured log ('item_archive_not_found', item_id=item_id) using get_logger() so the correlation id is bound; consider a dedicated counter app_item_archive_not_found_total so 4xx business errors are observable independently of the 5xx error_rate_pct SLO.

findings.message: No SLO threshold is defined for this new write endpoint. default_slos() only covers global request_latency_p99_ms and error_rate_pct — a slow or failing archive will be masked by aggregate latency/error metrics across all routes. Per the spec, new endpoints (especially state-mutating ones) should have a declared SLO.
findings.suggestion: Either add a per-route SLO (e.g. archive_latency_p99_ms with a PromQL mapping in SLO_PROMQL) or document that this endpoint is intentionally covered by the global SLO; if the former, also wire a route-labeled latency metric so the SLO PromQL has something to evaluate against.

findings.message: Inline `from fastapi import HTTPException` inside the function body — not an observability finding per se, but it is in the error path and indicates the error branch was added without going through the project's standard error-handling middleware. Confirm register_exception_handlers covers HTTPException with correlation-id logging; if not, the 404 path will bypass the unified error log/trace contract.
findings.suggestion: Move the import to module top-level and verify middleware/errors.py logs HTTPException with the bound correlation id; if it only logs unhandled exceptions, extend it (or log explicitly at the raise site) so 4xx business errors are not silently dropped from structured logs.
